notes.content: All transfer applicants must meet the University of California admissions eligibility requirements and the campus’s admission selection criteria. All requirements must be completed by the end of the spring term prior to transfer. For more information on UC admissions eligibility and admission to UC Santa Cruz, visit the Admissions website . The UC Santa Cruz Transfer Admission Guarantee (TAG) is a formal agreement that guarantees your fall admission to your chosen major, provided you are transferring from a California Community College and meet specific requirements. Visit the UC Santa Cruz TAG webpage for more information.
notes.contentHtml:
All transfer applicants must meet the University of California admissions eligibility requirements and the campus’s admission selection criteria. All requirements must be completed by the end of the spring term prior to transfer. For more information on UC admissions eligibility and admission to UC Santa Cruz, visit the Admissions website.


The UC Santa Cruz Transfer Admission Guarantee (TAG) is a formal agreement that guarantees your fall admission to your chosen major, provided you are transferring from a California Community College and meet specific requirements. Visit the UC Santa Cruz TAG webpage for more information.

notes.content: This is a non-screening major: completion of specific major coursework is not required for admission. However, students are strongly encouraged to complete as many lower-division courses as possible from the " Recommended Preparation " section below to support timely degree completion. Students applying to non-screening majors are also encouraged to complete the California General Education Transfer Curriculum (Cal-GETC) or all UC Santa Cruz general education requirements prior to transfer. For guidance on incorporating Cal-GETC into your academic plan, consult your community college counselor.
notes.contentHtml:
This is a non-screening major: completion of specific major coursework is not required for admission. However, students are strongly encouraged to complete as many lower-division courses as possible from the "Recommended Preparation" section below to support timely degree completion.


Students applying to non-screening majors are also encouraged to complete the California General Education Transfer Curriculum (Cal-GETC) or all UC Santa Cruz general education requirements prior to transfer. For guidance on incorporating Cal-GETC into your academic plan, consult your community college counselor.

notes.content: Some Advanced Placement (AP), International Baccalaureate Higher Level (IBH), and Advanced Level (A-level) exams satisfy UCSC course requirements. UCSC's exam credit policies may be found on the Degree Progress Unit website .
notes.contentHtml: Some Advanced Placement (AP), International Baccalaureate Higher Level (IBH), and Advanced Level (A-level) exams satisfy UCSC course requirements. UCSC's exam credit policies may be found on the Degree Progress Unit website.

notes.content: The Earth and Planetary Sciences Department welcomes prospective transfer students who are prepared for the major. No major preparation courses are required prior to transfer; however, the following courses or their equivalents are recommended to ensure timely graduation: All of the following courses: ANTH 1: Introduction to Biological Anthropology ANTH 2: Introduction to Cultural Anthropology ANTH 3: Introduction to Archaeology One of the following options: MATH 11A: Calculus with Applications AND MATH 11B: Calculus with Applications MATH 19A: Calculus for Science, Engineering, and Mathematics AND MATH 19B: Calculus for Science, Engineering, and Mathematics Plus one of the following options: EART 5: California Geology AND EART 5L: California Geology Laboratory EART 10: How the Earth Works AND EART 10L: Geologic Principles Laboratory EART 20: Environmental Geology AND EART 20L: Environmental Geology Laboratory Five lower-division science courses taken from the following list (note the lecture course and its associated lab course count as one course towards the five): BIOL 20A: Cell and Molecular Biology BIOE 20B: Development and Physiology BIOE 20C: Ecology and Evolution CHEM 3A: General Chemistry CHEM 3B/3BL: General Chemistry and Lab CHEM 3C/3CL: General Chemistry and Lab CHEM 4A/4AL: Advanced General Chemistry: Molecular Structure and Reactivity and Lab CHEM 4B/4BL: Advanced General Chemistry: Molecular Structure and Reactivity and Lab PHYS 5A/5L: Introduction to Physics I and Introduction to Physics I Laboratory PHYS 5B/5M: Introduction to Physics II and Introduction to Physics II Laboratory PHYS 6A/6L: Introductory Physics I and Introductory Physics I Laboratory PHYS 6B/6M: Introductory Physics II and Introductory Physics II Laboratory Students are strongly encouraged to prioritize recommended major preparation courses and may additionally complete courses that fulfill UC Santa Cruz general education requirements before transferring as time allows. Please visit the Earth & Planetary Sciences Department website to learn more about this major. UC Santa Cruz General Catalog: Earth Sciences/Anthropology Combined Major B.A. Program Statement
notes.contentHtml:
The Earth and Planetary Sciences Department welcomes prospective transfer students who are prepared for the major. No major preparation courses are required prior to transfer; however, the following courses or their equivalents are recommended to ensure timely graduation:


All of the following courses:

  • ANTH 1: Introduction to Biological Anthropology
  • ANTH 2: Introduction to Cultural Anthropology
  • ANTH 3: Introduction to Archaeology


One of the following options:

  • MATH 11A: Calculus with Applications AND MATH 11B: Calculus with Applications
  • MATH 19A: Calculus for Science, Engineering, and Mathematics AND MATH 19B: Calculus for Science, Engineering, and Mathematics


Plus one of the following options:

  • EART 5: California Geology AND EART 5L: California Geology Laboratory
  • EART 10: How the Earth Works AND EART 10L: Geologic Principles Laboratory
  • EART 20: Environmental Geology AND EART 20L: Environmental Geology Laboratory


Five lower-division science courses taken from the following list (note the lecture course and its associated lab course count as one course towards the five):

  • BIOL 20A: Cell and Molecular Biology
  • BIOE 20B: Development and Physiology
  • BIOE 20C: Ecology and Evolution
  • CHEM 3A: General Chemistry
  • CHEM 3B/3BL: General Chemistry and Lab
  • CHEM 3C/3CL: General Chemistry and Lab
  • CHEM 4A/4AL: Advanced General Chemistry: Molecular Structure and Reactivity and Lab
  • CHEM 4B/4BL: Advanced General Chemistry: Molecular Structure and Reactivity and Lab
  • PHYS 5A/5L: Introduction to Physics I and Introduction to Physics I Laboratory
  • PHYS 5B/5M: Introduction to Physics II and Introduction to Physics II Laboratory
  • PHYS 6A/6L: Introductory Physics I and Introductory Physics I Laboratory
  • PHYS 6B/6M: Introductory Physics II and Introductory Physics II Laboratory


Students are strongly encouraged to prioritize recommended major preparation courses and may additionally complete courses that fulfill UC Santa Cruz general education requirements before transferring as time allows.


Please visit the Earth & Planetary Sciences Department website to learn more about this major.

UC Santa Cruz General Catalog: Earth Sciences/Anthropology Combined Major B.A. Program Statement
